# Implementation Plan: Instagram Post Builder

**Branch**: `001-instagram-post-builder` | **Date**: 2026-04-29 | **Spec**: [spec.md](spec.md)
**Input**: Feature specification from `/specs/001-instagram-post-builder/spec.md`

**Note**: This plan was generated by the `/speckit-plan` command. See `.specify/templates/plan-template.md` for the execution workflow.

## Summary

Build a drag-and-drop Instagram template builder for the web that pre-loads Stitch colors and company fonts (Space Grotesk, Inter). Users create, reuse, and edit 1080x1080 post templates via drag-and-drop canvas interface, with live preview and PNG/JPG export. MVP covers template creation (P1) and reuse (P1); editing (P2) and export/sharing (P3) are deferred. Templates stored per-user in Supabase.

## Technical Context

**Language/Version**: TypeScript 5 (Next.js 16 App Router, React 19)  
**Primary Dependencies**: Next.js 16, React 19, HTML5 Canvas API, Tailwind CSS 4, Supabase client (@supabase/supabase-js), Framer Motion  
**Storage**: Supabase (PostgreSQL backend) — templates table with user_id, template_config JSON, created_at, updated_at  
**Testing**: Jest + React Testing Library for component/integration tests; Cypress for E2E canvas interactions  
**Target Platform**: Web browsers (Chrome, Firefox, Safari, Edge 2021+) with Canvas/SVG + drag-and-drop API support  
**Project Type**: Web application — single-page React component + API route for image generation  
**Performance Goals**: Drag-and-drop element repositioning <100ms latency; canvas preview updates <50ms; image export <5s  
**Constraints**: Output image exactly 1080x1080 px; template JSON <5MB; drag-and-drop must work on mobile (touch + mouse)  
**Scale/Scope**: Single user MVP; per-user template storage; templates shared globally (P3); concurrent users expected <1000 at launch

## Constitution Check

*GATE: Must pass before Phase 0 research. Re-check after Phase 1 design.*

**✅ PASS** — Feature aligns with constitution:

1. **Spec-First Development**: ✅ Spec is APROBADA with zero PENDIENTE items; ready for planning
2. **Verifiable Acceptance Criteria**: ✅ All 4 user stories define testable acceptance scenarios
3. **Frontend-First, User-Centric**: ✅ Feature is pure React/Next.js; prioritizes drag-and-drop UX
4. **Internationalization as Architecture**: ⚠️ **DESIGN REQUIREMENT**: Template builder UI (buttons, labels, placeholders) must use i18n keys (dict.{es,ca,en}.json) for all user-facing text. Post content (user-entered text) is user-provided and not translated.
5. **Integration Testing Over Mocks**: ✅ Canvas interactions, drag-and-drop, and template persistence will be integration-tested against real Supabase storage; external APIs mocked only where necessary

**⚠️ Note**: Constitution principle IV (i18n) applies to the builder UI. All UI strings must be in `src/i18n/dict/`. This is documented as a Design Requirement in Phase 1.

## Project Structure

### Documentation (this feature)

```text
specs/[###-feature]/
├── plan.md              # This file (/speckit-plan command output)
├── research.md          # Phase 0 output (/speckit-plan command)
├── data-model.md        # Phase 1 output (/speckit-plan command)
├── quickstart.md        # Phase 1 output (/speckit-plan command)
├── contracts/           # Phase 1 output (/speckit-plan command)
└── tasks.md             # Phase 2 output (/speckit-tasks command - NOT created by /speckit-plan)
```

### Source Code (repository root)

```text
src/
├── components/
│   ├── InstagramPostBuilder.tsx       # Main builder component
│   ├── Canvas/
│   │   ├── TemplateCanvas.tsx         # Canvas rendering + drag-and-drop
│   │   ├── ElementDragger.tsx         # Drag handler HOC
│   │   └── Preview.tsx                # Live preview pane
│   ├── Sidebar/
│   │   ├── ElementPalette.tsx         # Draggable elements (text, images)
│   │   ├── ColorPicker.tsx            # Pre-loaded Stitch palette
│   │   └── FontSelector.tsx           # Pre-loaded fonts (Space Grotesk, Inter)
│   ├── TemplateLibrary.tsx            # List saved templates, load/delete
│   └── ExportModal.tsx                # Download image dialog
├── services/
│   ├── templateService.ts            # Supabase CRUD for templates
│   ├── canvasRenderer.ts             # Canvas 2D drawing, 1080x1080 image export
│   └── designTokens.ts               # Stitch palette + fonts (from globals.css)
├── hooks/
│   ├── useTemplateBuilder.ts         # State for canvas, elements, colors
│   ├── useDragAndDrop.ts             # Drag-and-drop logic
│   └── useTemplateStorage.ts         # Load/save templates from Supabase
├── app/
│   └── instagram-builder/
│       └── page.tsx                  # Route: /instagram-builder

__tests__/
├── integration/
│   ├── template-create.test.tsx      # Create + save template (real Supabase)
│   ├── template-reuse.test.tsx       # Load + edit existing template
│   ├── drag-and-drop.test.tsx        # Canvas drag-and-drop interactions
│   └── image-export.test.tsx         # PNG/JPG export validation
└── unit/
    ├── canvasRenderer.test.ts        # Image rendering (1080x1080 validation)
    └── templateService.test.ts       # CRUD operations (mock Supabase)
```

**Structure Decision**: Web application feature integrated into existing Next.js app. UI components colocated in `src/components/` with feature-specific subdirectories. Services for Supabase + canvas rendering. Hooks for state management. Tests split into integration (real Supabase) and unit (mocked). No separate backend needed; canvas rendering happens client-side; image export uses HTML5 Canvas → blob.
